title: Banara leucothyrsa
intro: Banara leucothyrsa, also known as the White-veined Banara, is a plant species belonging to the family Salicaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential applications in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Banara leucothyrsa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations made from its leaves and stems include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as extracts used in the production of pharmaceuticals and nutritional supplements.

It belongs to the genus Banara, which is a small group of trees and shrubs native to Central and South America. The Salicaceae family is a part of the larger clade Rosidae, which comprises over 90,000 species of flowering plants.
